name: Unity Ads Mobile Monetization API
what_it_does: Mobile game advertising and monetization platform with REST API — enabling Unity game developers to monetize games with rewarded video, interstitials, and banners via Unity Ads SDK, acquire users through the Unity Ads network, and access cross-promotion within the Unity developer ecosystem.
use_cases: Integrating Unity Ads SDK for rewarded video monetization in Unity mobile games
Managing Unity Ads campaigns and budgets via REST API for user acquisition
Pulling ad revenue and fill rate data via Unity Ads Monetization API for reporting
Configuring ad placements and frequency caps programmatically via Unity Ads API
Accessing Unity developer network for cross-promotion between Unity games
not_for: Non-Unity apps (Unity Ads SDK is optimized for Unity game engine)
Web or desktop advertising (Unity Ads is mobile-native)
Non-gaming mobile apps (Unity Ads performs best for gaming audiences)
best_when: A mobile game developer using Unity engine wants seamlessly integrated in-game advertising monetization and user acquisition from the same ecosystem — especially for indie and mid-size game studios already on Unity.
avoid_when: You're building a non-game app, using a different game engine (Unreal, Godot), or need sophisticated programmatic demand beyond what Unity's managed network offers.

auth.notes: Unity project API key and service account key. Bearer token auth. Project-scoped access. No webhooks from Unity Ads — must poll for reporting. Unity SDK uses game ID and placement ID for in-app ad serving. MMP integration (Adjust, AppsFlyer) for attribution postbacks. No OAuth2 for Unity Ads API.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Unity Ads monetization free (revenue share model)
pricing.paid_tiers: CPI/ROAS-based pricing for UA; monetization is revenue share on ads served
pricing.notes: San Francisco company (Unity Technologies). Founded 2004. NYSE listed (U). $1.8B+ revenue (2023). Unity engine powers 70% of top mobile games. Merged with ironSource 2022. Unity Ads is part of Unity Grow division. 750,000+ game developers in Unity ecosystem. Deep engine integration is key differentiator — Unity Ads works natively in Unity without additional SDK. Significant controversy over Unity Runtime Fee (2023) — partially rolled back but damaged developer trust.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API for campaigns, monetization, and analytics. API key auth. Good but fragmented documentation across Unity Developer Portal, Unity Ads, and Unity Grow. SDK (Unity editor integration) is primary integration path. No webhooks — polling required. ironSource/Unity Grow consolidation still in progress — documentation may reference both systems.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Report reads fully idempotent. Campaign reads idempotent. Campaign creation not idempotent — check by name before creating.
known_agent_gotchas: Documentation fragmented across Unity Developer Portal, Unity Ads docs, and Unity Grow
ironSource merger (2022) created platform consolidation — some LevelPlay docs for same product
Unity Runtime Fee controversy (2023) damaged developer trust — evaluate alternatives
COPPA: games targeting children cannot use behavioral targeting or IDFA
No webhooks — must poll for reporting data; plan for async report generation

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. GDPR, CCPA, COPPA compliant. US and EU data residency. API key auth. Mobile game player data collected via SDK — GDPR data processing agreement required. COPPA: strict prohibition on behavioral data for children's games. Unity SDK collects device and gameplay data — implement privacy-by-design in game data collection.
